nutrient_source.title: Fruit punch (ready-to-drink, sweetened) and passion fruit juice nutritional data; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — Tea, ready-to-drink, sweetened; passion fruit juice, raw
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct USDA or HPB entry exists for KOI Thé Summer Passion. Values estimated by compositing: (1) USDA FDC #174856 — Beverages, tea, ready-to-drink, sweetened (approx. 8 kcal/100 g, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 2 g carbohydrate, 1.8 g sugar, 0 g fibre, 2 mg sodium); (2) USDA FDC #167762 — Passion-fruit juice, raw (approx. 51 kcal/100 g, 0.4 g protein, 0.1 g fat, 13.6 g carbohydrate, 11.2 g sugar, 0.2 g fibre, 6 mg sodium). A medium KOI cup (~500 ml) is estimated to contain roughly 60–70% sweetened tea base and 30–40% passion fruit component (syrup/juice). Blended estimate per 100 g: ~25 kcal, 0.1 g protein, 0.0 g fat, 6.3 g carbohydrate, 5.5 g sugar, 0.1 g fibre, 3 mg sodium. Values are conservative mid-range estimates and should be verified against KOI Thé nutritional disclosure when available.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — fruit juice and sweetened tea beverages; passion fruit GI reference
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for KOI Thé Summer Passion. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous sweetened fruit tea beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database: sweetened fruit juice/tea drinks typically range GI 40–55. Passion fruit juice has a low GI (~30–40) due to organic acids slowing glucose absorption; added sugar syrup raises the blended GI. A conservative mid-range estimate of 45 is applied. Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (45 × 31.5 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 14.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~3 mg/100 g; ~15 mg per 500 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: The Summer Passion fruit tea is very low in sodium and poses minimal direct risk for hypertension; however, individuals should be mindful of total sugar intake from sweetened beverages, which may contribute to weight gain and indirectly elevate blood pressure over time.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar content and glycemic load (~14 per medium serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: A medium serving of Summer Passion fruit tea delivers approximately 31.5 g of carbohydrate (predominantly added sugars) with a glycemic load of ~14, which is moderate; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request reduced or no sugar and limit portion frequency to manage postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and saturated fat; moderate sugar load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The beverage contains essentially no fat or saturated fat, posing low direct cardiovascular risk from lipid pathways; however, regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with elevated triglycerides and cardiovascular risk, so moderation and sugar-reduction options are advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Summer Passion (中-夏日百香, M size) is a Taiwanese-style passion fruit tea sold at KOI Thé, a popular bubble tea chain with outlets in Singapore including Funan Mall. The drink is prepared by combining a brewed green tea or jasmine tea base with passion fruit syrup or fresh passion fruit pulp and sugar syrup, then served chilled over ice in a medium-sized cup of approximately 500 ml. The flavour profile is bright, tangy, and tropical, with the characteristic floral tartness of passion fruit balanced by the mild astringency of the tea base and sweetness from added syrup. The texture is light and refreshing, without any milk or tapioca pearls in the standard formulation. Nutritionally, the beverage is low in energy (~25 kcal/100 g, ~125 kcal per 500 ml serving at standard sweetness), contains negligible protein and fat, and derives most of its caloric content from added sugars (~5.5 g sugar/100 g). Sodium is very low (~3 mg/100 g). Passion fruit contributes small amounts of vitamin C and potassium. The glycemic index is estimated at 45 (low GI category), but the glycemic load per medium serving is approximately 14 (moderate), making it a beverage that individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume with caution and preferably at reduced sweetness. The drink is dairy-free, vegan, and gluten-free, making it suitable for a wide range of dietary preferences. It is a common afternoon or post-meal beverage choice in Singapore's hawker and mall food-court culture.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry for KOI Thé Summer Passion exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any other approved primary source. Values are estimated by compositing USDA FDC data for sweetened ready-to-drink tea and raw passion fruit juice in proportions consistent with a typical Taiwanese-style fruit tea formulation. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database category for sweetened fruit tea beverages. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero — passion fruit tea beverages contain no fat by formulation; this is consistent with USDA FDC data for both sweetened tea and passion fruit juice components. 2. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all 0.0: confirmed zero by formulation for this beverage category. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are all -1: not reported in the composited USDA FDC source entries used for estimation; these micronutrients are present in trace amounts in passion fruit but are not reliably quantifiable from the available approved sources for this blended beverage. 4. GI set to 45 (low) as an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database sweetened fruit tea category; no direct GI measurement for KOI Summer Passion exists. Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (45 × 31.5) / 100 = 14.2. 5. Nutrient values should be updated when KOI Thé publishes official nutritional disclosure for Singapore. 6. Price SGD 5.80 preserved from input (field survey data, KOI Thé Funan, 1 restaurant). 7. Taiwanese cuisine multilingual naming applied: Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names provided following Singaporean dish multilingual rules, as this beverage is sold in Singapore by a Taiwanese-origin chain.
